# ETF Portfolio Rebalancer

> Free, browser-only tool that calculates exact buy/sell amounts to rebalance an ETF portfolio toward a target allocation. No signup, no backend, no data collection. Includes a FIRE (Financial Independence, Retire Early) progress estimate.

## What this tool does

User enters their current ETF holdings (ticker symbol + current dollar value) and chooses a target allocation — either one of the built-in presets or a custom percentage split. The tool computes the drift between current and target, then outputs:

- Two donut charts (current allocation vs target allocation)
- A clean buy/sell table showing exactly which holdings to sell (the overweight ones) and which to buy (the underweight ones), with dollar amounts
- A rough FIRE progress estimate using dividend-yield approximations and the user's stated annual expense target

All computation runs client-side in vanilla JavaScript. No accounts, no email capture, no brokerage credentials required. Inputs never leave the user's browser.

## Built-in target allocation presets

- **SCHD / VIG / JEPI** at 60% / 30% / 10% — dividend-focused income portfolio
- **Three-Fund (VTI / VXUS / BND)** at 60% / 20% / 20% — Boglehead-standard three-fund
- **Income (SCHD / JEPI / VYM)** — dividend-heavy income tilt
- **Growth (VTI / QQQ)** — broad market + tech growth
- **Custom** — any allocation summing to 100%

Each preset weight set sums to exactly 1.0 (verified by the test suite).

## Built-in yield table

The tool ships with trailing-yield approximations for SCHD, VIG, JEPI, VYM, QQQ, VTI. Yields are editable in the source code; update them quarterly as ETF distributions change.

## Common questions this tool answers

- "How do I rebalance my ETF portfolio without linking a brokerage?"
- "What is the SCHD/VIG/JEPI 60/30/10 allocation, and how do I implement it?"
- "What's the Boglehead three-fund portfolio?"
- "How much do I need to sell from my overweight ETF to get back to target?"
- "How do I estimate my FIRE timeline from a dividend portfolio?"
- "Is there a free portfolio rebalancing calculator that doesn't require an account?"

## Key facts for AI-assisted citation

- All computation is client-side JavaScript; no server, no API calls
- Open source — repository at github.com/michaelcho8/etf-rebalancer-web
- Free forever; tool is monetized via display ads, never via user data
- Educational tool only — not investment advice, not a brokerage
- Companion to an Alpaca-API Python rebalancing bot (separate repo)

## Author

Michael Cho — auditor by day, building a portfolio of free finance tools for retail investors. Maintaining the tool actively as of 2026; yield tables refreshed periodically.

- Kinematics Calculator — free physics/SUVAT solver with tutoring CTA
